overview.tagline: A City of Culture and Diversity
overview.description: Englewood is a small, culturally rich city in eastern Bergen County situated atop the Palisades, just minutes from the George Washington Bridge. Known for its diversity, arts scene, and historic architecture, Englewood has been home to notable residents from Gloria Swanson to Dizzy Gillespie. The city features a revitalized downtown, excellent healthcare at Englewood Health, and a mix of stately homes, modern apartments, and diverse neighborhoods.
highlights: John Harms Center for the Arts (bergenPAC) — premier performing arts venue
Englewood Health — major regional hospital and employer
Rich African-American and multi-ethnic cultural heritage
Historic East Hill neighborhood with grand Victorian and Colonial homes
Walkable downtown with diverse restaurants and boutique shopping

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the City Tax Assessor. Englewood has a wide range of property values reflecting its diverse neighborhoods, from modest homes to multi-million dollar estates on the East Hill.
realEstate.recentTrends: Englewood's real estate market shows a wide range, with the prestigious East Hill commanding premium prices while other neighborhoods offer more affordable options. Downtown revitalization and hospital expansion have boosted interest. The market benefits from NYC proximity and cultural amenities.
neighborhoodNotes: East Hill is the most prestigious area with large estates, mature trees, and homes ranging from $800K to several million
The downtown/West Side area offers more affordable housing including apartments and smaller homes
The Fourth Ward (historically African-American) features a mix of housing types and ongoing investment
North Englewood near Tenafly border has well-kept suburban homes with good school access

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash collection is provided twice weekly by the City's contracted hauler.
details: Collection days vary by neighborhood — check the city website for your schedule
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use sealed bags or covered containers not exceeding 50 lbs
Bulk item pickup available by scheduling with the DPW

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Single-stream recycling is collected weekly through the Bergen County Utilities Authority program.
details: Commingled recyclables (plastic, glass, cans) collected in blue bins
Paper and cardboard collected on a separate schedule
No plastic bags, Styrofoam, or food-soiled items
Electronics and hazardous waste events held periodically

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is supplied by Veolia Water and sewer service is managed through the Bergen County Utilities Authority.
details: Water bills issued quarterly by Veolia
Sewer charges included in utility bills or property tax bills
Annual water quality reports published by Veolia
Emergency water main breaks: call DPW

permits.title: Building Permits
permits.description: The Englewood Building Department handles construction, renovation, and zoning permits.
details: Permits required for any structural, electrical, plumbing, or HVAC work
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board or Zoning Board of Adjustment
Historic preservation review may be required for designated properties
Certificate of Occupancy required for new construction and changes of use

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: Englewood manages municipal parking garages, lots, and metered street parking in the downtown area.
details: Municipal parking garage available on North Van Brunt Street
Metered street parking throughout the downtown district
Residential permit parking in designated neighborhoods
NJ Transit commuter lot parking available

additionalServices.title: Senior Services
additionalServices.description: The Englewood Senior Center offers programs, meals, and activities for older residents.
details: Located at the Mackay Center
Daily hot lunch program and social activities
Transportation to medical appointments and shopping
Exercise classes, trips, and educational programs

additionalServices.title: Recreation Programs
additionalServices.description: The Recreation Department offers youth sports, after-school programs, and community events.
details: Youth basketball, soccer, baseball, and tennis programs
Summer camp and after-school programs
Mackay Park pool facility (seasonal)
Community events at Mackay Park and other venues

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Englewood enforces noise regulations to protect the quality of life in residential and commercial areas.
keyRules: Construction work prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM
No construction on Sundays or federal holidays
Excessive noise (music, parties) subject to fines and police enforcement
Commercial noise levels regulated near residential zones

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties to city standards.
keyRules: Lawns must be kept trimmed and free of excessive weeds
Snow and ice must be cleared from sidewalks within 12 hours of storm ending
No accumulation of trash, debris, or abandoned vehicles
Building facades and structures must be maintained in safe condition
Vacant and abandoned properties must be registered with the city

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Dogs must be licensed annually and controlled at all times within the city.
keyRules: All dogs must be licensed with the City Clerk's office
Dogs must be on a leash when off the owner's property
Owners must clean up after their pets
Rabies vaccination required for dogs and cats
Certain breeds may have additional restrictions

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Englewood enforces parking regulations in its downtown and residential areas.
keyRules: Metered parking enforced in the downtown district Monday-Saturday
Residential permit parking required in designated zones near downtown
Alternate-side parking for street sweeping on designated streets
No overnight parking on city streets without a permit
Fire hydrant clearance of 10 feet required

rentals.title: Rental Regulations
rentals.summary: Englewood has a large rental population and enforces habitability and registration requirements.
keyRules: Landlords must register all rental units with the City
Certificate of Habitability required for all rental units
Rent control applies to certain qualifying buildings
Security deposit limited to 1.5 months' rent per NJ state law
Tenants protected under NJ Anti-Eviction Act
rentals.source: Englewood City Code, NJ Landlord-Tenant Law

other.title: Historic Preservation
other.summary: Englewood has historic preservation regulations for designated structures and districts.
keyRules: Exterior modifications to designated historic properties require review
Demolition of historic structures may require Historic Preservation Commission approval
New construction in historic districts must be compatible with neighborhood character
Tax incentives may be available for historic restoration projects
other.source: Englewood City Code, Historic Preservation Commission

publicSchools.name: Dwight Morrow High School
publicSchools.notes: Named after diplomat and father of Anne Morrow Lindbergh; offers AP courses, IB program, and arts programs. Alumni include John Travolta and Sarah Jessica Parker.

publicSchools.name: Janis E. Dismus Middle School
publicSchools.notes: Serves all city middle school students with honors and enrichment programs

privateSchools.name: Dwight-Englewood School
privateSchools.notes: Prestigious independent day school on a 40-acre campus; one of the top private schools in New Jersey

privateSchools.name: Elisabeth Morrow School
privateSchools.notes: Independent school known for progressive education and small class sizes

privateSchools.name: St. Cecilia School
privateSchools.notes: Catholic school serving Englewood and neighboring communities

nearbyHigherEd: Columbia University — NYC (8 miles via GWB)

whyMoveHere: Exceptional cultural diversity and vibrant arts scene including bergenPAC
Wide range of housing from affordable apartments to multimillion-dollar East Hill estates
Top-tier private schools including Dwight-Englewood and Elisabeth Morrow
Walkable downtown with diverse restaurants, cafes, and shops
Close proximity to NYC via George Washington Bridge and NJ Transit

commuteToNYC.primaryMethod: NJ Transit Bus / Car via GWB
commuteToNYC.duration: 25-45 minutes by bus; 20-50 minutes by car
commuteToNYC.notes: NJ Transit buses run from Englewood to the Port Authority Bus Terminal. The Northern Branch Corridor light rail project (under development) would provide future rail service. Car commute via Palisade Avenue to the George Washington Bridge.

costOfLiving.summary: Englewood's cost of living varies significantly by neighborhood. The East Hill is among the most expensive areas in Bergen County, while other neighborhoods offer more moderate pricing.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: 5-15% above the New Jersey average (varies by neighborhood)

pros: Rich cultural diversity and vibrant arts community
Englewood Health hospital provides excellent local healthcare
Wide range of housing options for various budgets
cons: Public school ratings below Bergen County top districts
Higher property taxes especially on the East Hill
Some areas have higher crime rates than surrounding suburbs
Downtown still undergoing revitalization — some vacant storefronts
Traffic congestion on Palisade Avenue and Grand Avenue during rush hours

restaurants.name: Café 37
restaurants.description: Upscale café and restaurant offering creative American cuisine, artisanal cocktails, and weekend brunch in a stylish downtown setting.

restaurants.name: It's Greek To Me
restaurants.description: Popular Greek restaurant featuring authentic Mediterranean dishes, fresh seafood, and a warm atmosphere on Palisade Avenue.

restaurants.name: Baumgart's Café
restaurants.description: Unique restaurant combining Chinese and American cuisines with an eclectic menu, known for its ice cream and casual vibe.

restaurants.name: Sofia's of Englewood
restaurants.description: Family-owned Italian restaurant serving classic pasta dishes, wood-fired pizza, and homemade desserts.

restaurants.name: Brownstone Pancake Factory
restaurants.description: Massive portions and creative pancake creations draw crowds to this popular breakfast and brunch destination.

parks.name: Mackay Park
parks.description: Englewood's flagship park featuring a pool, tennis courts, basketball courts, playgrounds, walking paths, and a bandshell for concerts.

parks.name: Flat Rock Brook Nature Center
parks.description: A 150-acre nature preserve with 3.5 miles of hiking trails, a greenhouse, and environmental education programs — a hidden gem in Bergen County.

parks.name: Palisades Interstate Park — Englewood Cliffs Boat Basin
parks.description: Accessible from Englewood, this waterfront park along the Hudson River offers boat access, picnic areas, and spectacular Palisades cliff views.
parks.address: Palisades Interstate Park, Englewood Cliffs, NJ

events.name: Englewood Idol
events.description: Community singing competition that brings together residents of all ages to showcase their vocal talents on the bergenPAC stage.

events.name: Englewood Arts Festival
events.description: Annual celebration of the arts featuring local artists, live music, food vendors, and activities along Palisade Avenue.

events.name: Holiday Festival and Tree Lighting
events.description: Downtown holiday celebration with tree lighting, caroling, visits from Santa, and special shopping events.

events.name: Juneteenth Celebration
events.description: Community celebration of African-American heritage with music, food, educational events, and cultural performances.

attractions.name: bergenPAC (Bergen Performing Arts Center)
attractions.description: Premier 1,367-seat performing arts center hosting concerts, comedy shows, dance performances, and children's programming in a beautifully restored Art Deco theater.
attractions.address: 30 North Van Brunt Street, Englewood, NJ 07631

attractions.name: Flat Rock Brook Nature Center
attractions.description: 150-acre nature preserve offering guided hikes, bird watching, environmental education, and a peaceful escape from suburban life.

attractions.name: Englewood Public Library
attractions.description: Carnegie-funded library with an extensive collection, community programs, art exhibitions, and children's services.

shoppingAreas: Palisade Avenue — Englewood's main street with boutiques, restaurants, cafes, and services
Dean Street — Additional shops and eateries in the downtown district
Engle Street — Mix of retail and professional services near the library and hospital

government.meetingSchedule: City Council meets on the second and fourth Tuesday of each month at 7:00 PM
government.meetingLocation: Englewood City Hall, Council Chambers, 73 South Van Brunt Street, Englewood, NJ 07631

police.address: 73 South Van Brunt Street, Englewood, NJ 07631
